title: My Shadow is Purple
description: My Shadow is Purple is a fiction by Scott Stuart. Banned for attempting to promote homosexual culture in Malaysia, which goes against religious and cultural sensitivities in the country.
descriptionParagraphs: My Shadow is Purple is a fiction by Scott Stuart. Banned for attempting to promote homosexual culture in Malaysia, which goes against religious and cultural sensitivities in the country.

Its interest lies partly in the way literary or informational writing gets collapsed into a public-morality problem. As a fiction, it can be read not only for subject matter but for the way form, tone, and circulation make a text feel dangerous, intimate, or politically usable to anxious officials.

It also matters as part of a wider censorship history in Malaysia. The present page is a dossier starter built from source-tracked ban records; the surviving note currently says: Banned for attempting to promote homosexual culture in Malaysia, which goes against religious and cultural sensitivities in the country. The home ministry said these books have been banned under Section 7(1) of Act 301. More publication history, translations, and close reading can be added later.

overview.englishDescription: My Shadow is Purple is a fiction by Scott Stuart. Banned for attempting to promote homosexual culture in Malaysia, which goes against religious and cultural sensitivities in the country.
banningParagraphs: My Shadow is Purple entered censorship debates as a fiction associated with morality, print scandal, and sexuality. In the current dossier, the main state objections cluster around obscenity and public morality.

The earliest event currently captured here is Date not yet pinned down in Malaysia, where Malaysia authorities banned publication or circulation. Banned for attempting to promote homosexual culture in Malaysia, which goes against religious and cultural sensitivities in the country. The home ministry said these books have been banned under Section 7(1) of Act 301. Banned for attempting to promote homosexual culture in Malaysia, which goes against religious and cultural sensitivities in the country. The home ministry said these books have been banned under Section 7(1) of Act 301 as they are considered 'undesirable.

counterReadings.title: Banned Books: Literature Suppressed on Sexual Grounds
counterReadings.author: Dawn B. Sova
counterReadings.note: Surveys the legal and moral language used to suppress books as obscene.

counterReadings.title: Banned Books: 387 B.C. to 1978 A.D.
counterReadings.author: Anne Lyon Haight
counterReadings.note: Useful for seeing how obscenity law and censorship habits changed over time.

counterReadings.title: 100 Banned Books: Censorship Histories of World Literature
counterReadings.author: Nicholas J. Karolides, Margaret Bald, and Dawn B. Sova
counterReadings.note: A compact reference on how censorship systems moved across states, churches, and courts.
